mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand as the real discount rate rises above historical norms. This compression of present values of future corporate earnings drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) serves as a continuous signal of labor market momentum. Sustained payroll growth above 150,000 persons per month indicates robust demand and supports equity valuations through higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between lagged PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward SPX returns is < 0.10 over any 3-year rolling window, or mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of months with payroll growth > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand. When both series decline simultaneously by > 2% month-over-month, this indicates demand contraction that portends earnings downgrades and equity multiple compression within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 722,708M and HOUST at 1,501k as of 2025-02-15
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or demand shock regime
failure_condition: Joint decline signal produces negative 5-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines for 2+ consecutive months, this signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressures that reduce equity valuations. Negative 20-day returns follow such supply contractions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances following 2+ month INDPRO decline, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.2% year-over-year signals persistent inflation that constrains Federal Reserve policy flexibility and raises real discount rates. This mechanism predicts negative 60-day equity returns in high-inflation regimes where monetary policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of months where CPI YoY > 3.2%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure labor market tightness. When UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in a 3-month window while PAYEMS growth slows below 100k, this signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS growth < 100k in latest month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159,398k as of 2025-03-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession-onset regime
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) serves as the broadest demand signal. When quarterly GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized, this indicates demand weakness that compresses equity earnings multiples. Negative 60-day forward returns follow weak GDP growth quarters.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% in latest quarter
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of quarters with GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) together measure consumer and construction demand momentum. When both series show sustained growth (> 2% MoM for 2+ consecutive months), this signals robust demand that supports equity earnings growth and produces positive 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST growth > 2% MoM AND RSAFS growth > 2% MoM for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,501k and RSAFS at 722,708M as of 2025-02-15
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion or post-shock recovery regime
failure_condition: Joint growth signal produces positive 5-day returns in < 56% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year window.
